methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Longshot Space is a Seed-stage space and aerospace company founded by Mike Grace and Nathan Saichek that has raised $10M+. The valuation universe includes 5 public comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, though same-stage comparables within the sector are sparse.

The Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable approach given data constraints. Using total raised of $10M as a valuation proxy and applying a standard Seed-to-next-stage multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $25M. The Public-Comp Anchoring method cannot be applied absent disclosed revenue; the Space & Aerospace sector median P/S of 25.8x across the 6 public comps is directionally relevant but not actionable without target financials. The Peer-Median approach fails due to insufficient same- or adjacent-stage private comparables meeting filtering criteria—while 11 same-sector peers exist, none satisfy the narrow peer set threshold of ≥3 datapoints.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $21M–$29M, with $25M as the median point. This range reflects moderate step-up expectations from seed capital deployment. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue data, which limits comparability anchoring to public comps, and the thinness of same-stage private peer data in frontier aerospace. Any revision would require either disclosed financial metrics or additional comparable transactions at similar capital and stage.
